content:
- completed the dynamic-source task end to end: fixed local endpoint publication in DCS, tightened HA recovery/start semantics, suppressed redundant HA process dispatch, and made StartPostgres idempotent when postmaster is already running
- all required gates passed in this workspace: make check, make test, make test-long, make lint
- updated the task file acceptance criteria with passes=true and ran .ralph/task_switch.sh; next is git add/commit/push with a task-finished message

- Read .ralph/current_tasks.md and .ralph/current_tasks_done.md after loading prior progress.
- Resolved selection precedence: active unblocked failing bug `.ralph/tasks/bugs/ha-api-polls-hang-during-no-quorum.md` exists and has no blockers, so it outranks all other tasks.
- Next step was to write that exact task path into .ralph/current_task.txt and quit immediately per Choose-Task instructions.

- read prior progress log entry from codex memory
- next step is to inspect .ralph/current_task.txt and the referenced task file to see whether this turn is plan, verify, or execute
- need to follow the task-file phase marker exactly and keep writing progress as I go

- current task points to .ralph/tasks/bugs/ha-api-polls-hang-during-no-quorum.md
- task file is still in initial not_started state with no plan, so this turn should only research enough to write the first detailed plan and end with TO BE VERIFIED
- investigating failing test name and HA API state handler next

- traced the likely root cause: node runtime is current_thread and both dcs::worker and ha::apply call sync DcsStore methods that block via recv_timeout(2s)
- under no quorum that can stall the entire runtime and can also delay FailSafe publication because ha::worker publishes state only after effect dispatch
- writing a detailed draft plan into the task file now, ending at TO BE VERIFIED per workflow

- wrote Draft 1 full implementation plan into the ha-api-polls-hang-during-no-quorum task file
- plan now ends with TO BE VERIFIED as required, with explicit root-cause hypothesis and execution phases
- stopping here without implementation so the next pass can do the mandatory skeptical review and alter the plan

- read the current task file and saw it was already at the mandatory TO BE VERIFIED phase
- did a skeptical review against the actual code paths in src/bin/pgtuskmaster.rs, src/runtime/node.rs, src/dcs/worker.rs, src/ha/worker.rs, src/ha/apply.rs, src/api/worker.rs, src/api/controller.rs, and the no-quorum long-test helper
- changed the plan materially: removed the broad async DcsStore adapter and async API widening from the first-pass plan, replaced them with an explicit multi-thread node runtime fix plus publish-before-dispatch ordering in ha::worker, tightened the long test to require API observability, and replaced the task marker with NOW EXECUTE
- important finding: relying on Tokio multi-thread defaults would be too weak because a single-core environment could still leave one worker thread, so the verified plan now calls for an explicit worker_threads count > 1
- stopping here on purpose because this turn was the plan-review pass; next turn should execute exactly the verified NOW EXECUTE plan without re-exploring

- read the current task file and confirmed it already ends with NOW EXECUTE
- switching from planning to implementation; next reading the precise runtime, ha worker, and no-quorum test files named in the plan
- need to implement with minimal surface: explicit multi-thread runtime, publish-before-dispatch in ha worker, stricter long-test API observability, then run all gates

- patched the real node binary to use a multi-thread Tokio runtime with an explicit 4 worker threads
- changed ha::worker::step_once to publish the selected HA state before effect dispatch and publish a follow-up faulted state only if dispatch errors occur
- added focused blocking regressions in ha worker tests and worker contract tests, tightened the no-quorum scenario helper to require all-nodes API observability, and updated the relevant docs

- enabled tokio rt-multi-thread in Cargo.toml because both the real node runtime fix and the new multi-thread regressions require that feature
- targeted regressions now pass: ha::worker blocking release-leader publish-order test and the API/debug/HA responsiveness contract test
- next running the specific no-quorum e2e before the full make check/test/test-long/lint gates

- make check and make test passed; make test-long found a regression in e2e_no_quorum_fencing_blocks_post_cutoff_commits_and_preserves_integrity
- logs pointed to stale DCS trust recovery and cluster churn during prolonged no-quorum, so I tightened DcsTrust evaluation to require fresh member/leader records before returning FullQuorum
- hit a transient cargo link artifact issue in target/debug while rerunning the failing test; cleaning and rerunning now

- Resumed from context compaction and confirmed the in-flight make check finished successfully.
- Proceeding with full gate sequence again starting from make test.
- If a gate fails, I need to fix the regression and rerun the sequence before touching the task completion flow.

- make test completed successfully after the trust freshness and publish-before-dispatch changes.
- Starting make test-long, the main end-to-end confirmation for the no-quorum/failsafe behavior.
- Need to watch especially for the integrity-preservation and strict all-node API responsiveness scenarios.

- make test-long passed, including the long HA no-quorum scenarios that previously regressed.
- Proceeding to make lint as the last required gate before task completion bookkeeping.
- After lint passes, update the bug task file with completed checkboxes and passes=true, then switch task, commit, and push.

- All required gates passed: make check, make test, make test-long, make lint.
- Updated the bug task file to completed/passes=true and recorded execution notes plus verification evidence.
- Running task_switch next, then I need to stage all changes, commit with the required message format, and push.
